title: Public Understanding of Factory Farming
description:
This is an exploratory study to determine how the U.S. general public understands the term “factory farming” and other relevant terms related to industrial animal agriculture, and their perceptions of these terms/concepts. Further, this study seeks to understand how different demographics and geographic regions of residence shape these understandings/perceptions. A survey will be conducted to answer the following research questions: 1) How does the U.S. general public understand the term "factory farming” and what perceptions do they have about it? 2) Are there notable variations in public understanding of “factory farming” based on key demographic distinctions or political affiliations? 3) Are there alternative terms to “factory farming” that can more effectively convey animal advocacy messages?

The following are hypotheses of this exploratory study: 1) Members of the general public will have distinct understandings and associated perceptions of the term “factory farming” corresponding to different demographic factors such as age, gender, race, geographic location, and political affiliation. 2) The term “factory farming” is interpreted inconsistently and has different meanings to different people, suggesting there is an alternative term that may be more effective at conveying animal advocacy messages.

title: Globalizing The Factory Farm:  International Organizations And The Spread Of Industrial Animal Agriculture
description: This Faunalytics study explores the ways that development finance institutions, United Nations agencies, and others directly and indirectly support factory farming in low- and middle-income countries. With key insights from people working in and alongside these international organizations, advocates can ensure they are equipped with the most effective outreach strategies

title: Public Understanding of "Factory Farming"
description: This is an exploratory, survey-based study to determine how the U.S. general public understands the term “factory farming” and other relevant terms related to industrial animal agriculture, and their perceptions of these terms/concepts. Further, this study seeks to understand how different demographics and geographic regions of residence shape these understandings/perceptions.

title: Testing The Short-Term & Long-Term Impact Of Different Approaches To Advocacy
description: This is a randomized control trial investigating the short-term (two weeks post-exposure) and long-term (three months post-exposure) effects of animal advocacy tactics (news articles, op-eds, disruptive protests, sanctuary videos, street outreach videos, educational videos, undercover investigation videos, and recipe videos) compared with a control condition.

title: Food Systems Advocacy in the Global South: A Framework and Pilot in India
description: This exploratory study forms the first stage of a broader project to improve the cultural and strategic alignment of animal advocacy efforts across the Global South. It seeks to develop a practical framework that helps advocates and funders identify and integrate key contextual factors into campaign design. The project will begin with India as a pilot site, with potential for future replication in other countries.

title: Understanding How India’s Gen Z Shapes Family Food Choices
description: This study will employ an online survey targeting Gen Z young adults in India who currently live with their parents (which is a common practice in India). The objective is twofold: 1) to capture their attitudes toward plant-based eating, and 2) to understand how these Gen Z attitudes shape household decisions—particularly around food choices.

title: Public Acceptability Of Standard U.S. Animal Agriculture Practices
description: In this study, we will survey ~1,000 U.S. adults to assess their views on standard animal agriculture practices.

title: Political Animals? How U.S. Voters Respond To Candidates Making Farmed Animal Policy Proposals
description: This quantitative Faunalytics study examines the impact on vote choice and perceptions of political candidates when those candidates propose changes to U.S. food policy. It focuses on voter responses to policy proposals aimed at supporting or opposing industrial animal agriculture.

title: Going Vegan: A Review of Global Rates
description: This project addresses the lack of clear, standardized data on the global prevalence of vegetarianism and veganism. It includes a systematic review of academic and grey literature to synthesize global prevalence estimates, analyze trends over time, and assess the methodological discrepancies between self-reported dietary identity and measured dietary intake.

title: Quantifying The Small Body Problem: A Meta-Analysis Of Animal Product Reduction Interventions
description:
This research provides a meta-analysis to evaluate evidence of a substitution effect in interventions designed to
reduce or eliminate animal products from participant diets in which small-bodied animals are substituted for large-bodied animals.
